{
  "command": "@'\nimport { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from \"react\";\n\nimport { queryGraph } from \"../services/api\";\nimport type { ChatMessage } from \"../types/api\";\n\ninterface ChatPanelProps {\n  highlightedNodes: string[];\n  onFocusNode: (nodeId: string | null) => void;\n  onHighlightNodes: (nodeIds: string[]) => void;\n}\n\nfunction createMessageId() {\n  return `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`;\n}\n\nfunction pickPrimaryNodeId(rawData: unknown, relevantNodeIds: string[] | undefined): string | null {\n  const records =\n    rawData && typeof rawData === \"object\" && \"records\" in rawData && Array.isArray((rawData as { records?: unknown[] }).records)\n      ? (rawData as { records: unknown[] }).records\n      : [];\n\n  const firstRecord = records[0];\n  if (firstRecord && typeof firstRecord === \"object\") {\n    const candidate = firstRecord as Record<string, unknown>;\n    if (typeof candidate.node_id === \"string\") {\n      return candidate.node_id;\n    }\n    if (typeof candidate.entity_id === \"string\") {\n      return candidate.entity_id;\n    }\n    if (Array.isArray(candidate.chain) && candidate.chain.length > 0) {\n      const firstChainNode = candidate.chain[0];\n      if (firstChainNode && typeof firstChainNode === \"object\") {\n        const chainCandidate = firstChainNode as Record<string, unknown>;\n        if (typeof chainCandidate.node_id === \"string\") {\n          return chainCandidate.node_id;\n        }\n        if (typeof chainCandidate.entity_id === \"string\") {\n          return chainCandidate.entity_id;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  const firstRelevant = relevantNodeIds?.find((value) => Boolean(value));\n  return firstRelevant ?? null;\n}\n\nexport function ChatPanel({ highlightedNodes, onFocusNode, onHighlightNodes }: ChatPanelProps) {\n  const [input, setInput] = useState(\"\");\n  const [messages, setMessages] = useState<ChatMessage[]>([\n    {\n      id: createMessageId(),\n      role: \"assistant\",\n      text: \"Hi! I can help you analyze the Order to Cash process.\",\n    },\n  ]);\n  const [isThinking, setIsThinking] = useState(false);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const handleKeyDown = (event: KeyboardEvent) => {\n      if (event.key === \"Escape\") {\n        setInput(\"\");\n      }\n    };\n\n    window.addEventListener(\"keydown\", handleKeyDown);\n    return () => window.removeEventListener(\"keydown\", handleKeyDown);\n  }, []);\n\n  const activeHighlightCount = useMemo(() => highlightedNodes.length, [highlightedNodes.length]);\n\n  const handleSend = async () => {\n    const question = input.trim();\n    if (!question || isThinking) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const userMessage: ChatMessage = {\n      id: createMessageId(),\n      role: \"user\",\n      text: question,\n    };\n\n    setMessages((current) => [...current, userMessage]);\n    setInput(\"\");\n    setIsThinking(true);\n\n    try {\n      const response = await queryGraph(question);\n      const rejected = response.answer.includes(\"designed to answer questions related to the dataset only\");\n      const assistantMessage: ChatMessage = {\n        id: createMessageId(),\n        role: \"assistant\",\n        text: response.answer,\n        relevantNodeIds: response.relevant_node_ids,\n        rawData: response.raw_data,\n        rejected,\n      };\n      setMessages((current) => [...current, assistantMessage]);\n\n      const primaryNodeId = pickPrimaryNodeId(response.raw_data, response.relevant_node_ids);\n      onFocusNode(primaryNodeId);\n      onHighlightNodes(primaryNodeId ? [primaryNodeId] : []);\n    } catch (error) {\n      const message: ChatMessage = {\n        id: createMessageId(),\n        role: \"assistant\",\n        text: error instanceof Error ? error.message : \"Query failed. Please try again.\",\n      };\n      setMessages((current) => [...current, message]);\n      onFocusNode(null);\n      onHighlightNodes([]);\n    } finally {\n      setIsThinking(false);\n    }\n  };\n\n  return (\n    <aside className=\"ml-4 flex w-[430px] shrink-0 overflow-hidden rounded-[22px] border border-slate-200 bg-white shadow-sm\">\n      <div className=\"flex h-full min-h-0 w-full flex-col\">\n        <div className=\"border-b border-slate-200 px-5 py-4\">\n          <h2 className=\"text-[18px] font-semibold text-slate-950\">Chat with Graph</h2>\n          <p className=\"mt-1 text-sm text-slate-500\">Order to Cash</p>\n        </div>\n\n        <div className=\"border-b border-slate-100 px-5 py-5\">\n          <div className=\"flex items-start gap-4\">\n            <div className=\"flex size-12 shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-full bg-slate-950 text-lg font-semibold text-white\">\n              D\n            </div>\n            <div>\n              <h3 className=\"text-[18px] font-semibold text-slate-950\">Dodge AI</h3>\n              <p className=\"text-sm text-slate-500\">Graph Agent</p>\n            </div>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n\n        <div className=\"flex-1 space-y-4 overflow-auto px-5 py-5\">\n          {messages.map((message) => (\n            <div key={message.id} className=\"space-y-2\">\n              <div className={`text-[15px] leading-8 ${message.rejected ? \"text-amber-700\" : \"text-slate-900\"}`}>\n                {message.text}\n              </div>\n              {message.relevantNodeIds && message.relevantNodeIds.length > 0 ? (\n                <div className=\"text-xs text-slate-500\">\n                  Focused graph on {message.relevantNodeIds[0]}.\n                </div>\n              ) : null}\n            </div>\n          ))}\n          {isThinking ? <div className=\"text-[15px] text-slate-500\">Thinking through the graph...</div> : null}\n        </div>\n\n        <div className=\"border-t border-slate-200 p-5\">\n          <div className=\"mb-3 flex items-center gap-2 rounded-full border border-emerald-100 bg-emerald-50 px-4 py-2 text-sm text-emerald-700\">\n            <span className=\"size-2 rounded-full bg-emerald-500\" />\n            Dodge AI is awaiting instructions\n            <span className=\"ml-auto text-slate-400\">{activeHighlightCount > 0 ? `${activeHighlightCount} focused` : \"Ready\"}</span>\n          </div>\n          <div className=\"rounded-2xl border border-slate-200 bg-white p-4 shadow-sm\">\n            <textarea\n              className=\"h-28 w-full resize-none border-0 bg-transparent text-[15px] text-slate-900 outline-none placeholder:text-slate-400\"\n              onChange={(event) => setInput(event.target.value)}\n              onKeyDown={(event) => {\n                if (event.key === \"Enter\" && !event.shiftKey) {\n                  event.preventDefault();\n                  void handleSend();\n                }\n                if (event.key === \"Escape\") {\n                  setInput(\"\");\n                }\n              }}\n              placeholder=\"Analyze anything\"\n              value={input}\n            />\n            <div className=\"mt-4 flex items-center justify-end\">\n              <button\n                className=\"rounded-2xl bg-slate-500 px-6 py-3 text-sm font-medium text-white transition hover:bg-slate-600 disabled:opacity-60\"\n                disabled={isThinking || !input.trim()}\n                onClick={() => void handleSend()}\n                type=\"button\"\n              >\n                Send\n              </button>\n            </div>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n    </aside>\n  );\n}\n'@ | Set-Content frontend\\src\\components\\ChatPanel.tsx",

{
  "command": "@'\nimport { useEffect, useRef, useState } from \"react\";\n\nimport cytoscape, { type Core, type ElementDefinition, type NodeSingular } from \"cytoscape\";\nimport coseBilkent from \"cytoscape-cose-bilkent\";\n\nimport { fetchGraph, fetchGraphStats, fetchSubgraph } from \"../services/api\";\nimport type { CytoscapeNodeData, GraphPayload, GraphStats, NodeSummary } from \"../types/api\";\nimport { DEFAULT_NODE_COLOR, NODE_TYPE_COLORS } from \"../types/graph\";\n\ncytoscape.use(coseBilkent);\n\ninterface GraphViewerProps {\n  expandTarget: { nodeId: string; depth: number } | null;\n  focusedNodeId: string | null;\n  highlightedNodes: string[];\n  onClearHighlights: () => void;\n  onNodeSelect: (node: NodeSummary | null) => void;\n  refreshToken: number;\n}\n\ninterface GraphOverlayCardProps {\n  node: CytoscapeNodeData;\n  connections: number;\n}\n\nfunction toElements(payload: GraphPayload): ElementDefinition[] {\n  const nodes = payload.nodes.map((node) => ({ data: node.data }));\n  const edges = payload.edges.map((edge) => ({ data: edge.data }));\n  return [...nodes, ...edges];\n}\n\nfunction normalizeHighlightId(value: string): string {\n  const trimmed = value.trim();\n  const parts = trimmed.split(\":\");\n  return parts[parts.length - 1] ?? trimmed;\n}\n\nfunction buildNodeSummary(node: NodeSingular): NodeSummary {\n  return {\n    nodeId: node.data(\"entity_id\"),\n    nodeKey: node.id(),\n    entityId: node.data(\"entity_id\"),\n    entityType: node.data(\"entity_type\"),\n    label: node.data(\"label\") ?? node.data(\"entity_id\"),\n  };\n}\n\nfunction GraphOverlayCard({ node, connections }: GraphOverlayCardProps) {\n  const metadataEntries = Object.entries(node.metadata ?? {});\n  const visibleEntries = metadataEntries.slice(0, 14);\n  const hiddenCount = Math.max(metadataEntries.length - visibleEntries.length, 0);\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"absolute left-1/2 top-16 z-20 max-h-[420px] w-[320px] -translate-x-1/2 overflow-hidden rounded-[20px] border border-slate-200 bg-white p-4 shadow-2xl\">\n      <div className=\"mb-3\">\n        <h3 className=\"text-lg font-semibold text-slate-900\">{node.label ?? node.entity_id}</h3>\n        <p className=\"text-sm text-slate-500\">Entity: {node.entity_type}</p>\n      </div>\n\n      <div className=\"max-h-[300px] space-y-1 overflow-auto pr-1 text-sm text-slate-700\">\n        {visibleEntries.map(([key, value]) => (\n          <div key={key}>\n            <span className=\"font-medium text-slate-900\">{key}:</span>{\" \"}\n            <span className=\"break-words text-slate-600\">{typeof value === \"object\" ? JSON.stringify(value) : String(value)}</span>\n          </div>\n        ))}\n        {hiddenCount > 0 ? (\n          <div className=\"pt-1 text-xs italic text-slate-400\">Additional fields hidden for readability</div>\n        ) : null}\n      </div>\n\n      <div className=\"mt-3 border-t border-slate-100 pt-3 text-sm text-slate-600\">Connections: {connections}</div>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n\nexport function GraphViewer({\n  expandTarget,\n  focusedNodeId,\n  highlightedNodes,\n  onClearHighlights,\n  onNodeSelect,\n  refreshToken,\n}: GraphViewerProps) {\n  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement | null>(null);\n  const cyRef = useRef<Core | null>(null);\n  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);\n  const [stats, setStats] = useState<GraphStats | null>(null);\n  const [overlayNode, setOverlayNode] = useState<CytoscapeNodeData | null>(null);\n  const [overlayConnections, setOverlayConnections] = useState(0);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!containerRef.current || cyRef.current) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const cy = cytoscape({\n      container: containerRef.current,\n      elements: [],\n      layout: {\n        name: \"cose-bilkent\",\n        fit: true,\n        padding: 120,\n        nodeRepulsion: 240000,\n        idealEdgeLength: 180,\n        edgeElasticity: 0.12,\n        nestingFactor: 0.9,\n        gravity: 0.12,\n        tile: false,\n      } as cytoscape.LayoutOptions,\n      style: [\n        {\n          selector: \"node\",\n          style: {\n            \"background-color\": (element) => NODE_TYPE_COLORS[element.data(\"entity_type\")] ?? DEFAULT_NODE_COLOR,\n            label: \"\",\n            width: \"20\",\n            height: \"20\",\n            opacity: 0.95,\n            \"border-width\": 0,\n            \"overlay-opacity\": 0,\n          },\n        },\n        {\n          selector: \"edge\",\n          style: {\n            width: \"4px\",\n            \"line-color\": \"#8fd0ff\",\n            \"target-arrow-color\": \"#8fd0ff\",\n            \"target-arrow-shape\": \"none\",\n            \"curve-style\": \"straight\",\n            opacity: 0.45,\n          },\n        },\n        {\n          selector: \".active-node\",\n          style: {\n            width: \"24\",\n            height: \"24\",\n            \"background-color\": \"#111827\",\n          },\n        },\n        {\n          selector: \".active-edge\",\n          style: {\n            width: \"4.5px\",\n            opacity: 0.95,\n            \"line-color\": \"#38bdf8\",\n          },\n        },\n        {\n          selector: \".query-highlight\",\n          style: {\n            width: \"28\",\n            height: \"28\",\n            \"background-color\": \"#ef4444\",\n            \"underlay-color\": \"#fecaca\",\n            \"underlay-padding\": \"9px\",\n            \"underlay-opacity\": 0.95,\n          },\n        },\n      ],\n    });\n\n    cy.on(\"tap\", \"node\", (event) => {\n      const node = event.target;\n      cy.elements().removeClass(\"active-node active-edge query-highlight\");\n      node.addClass(\"active-node\");\n      node.connectedEdges().addClass(\"active-edge\");\n      setOverlayNode(node.data() as CytoscapeNodeData);\n      setOverlayConnections(node.connectedEdges().length);\n      onNodeSelect(buildNodeSummary(node));\n      onClearHighlights();\n    });\n\n    cy.on(\"tap\", (event) => {\n      if (event.target === cy) {\n        cy.elements().removeClass(\"active-node active-edge query-highlight\");\n        setOverlayNode(null);\n        setOverlayConnections(0);\n        onNodeSelect(null);\n        onClearHighlights();\n      }\n    });\n\n    cyRef.current = cy;\n\n    return () => {\n      cy.destroy();\n      cyRef.current = null;\n    };\n  }, [onClearHighlights, onNodeSelect]);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    let isActive = true;\n\n    async function loadGraph() {\n      setLoading(true);\n      setError(null);\n      try {\n        const [graphPayload, graphStats] = await Promise.all([\n          expandTarget ? fetchSubgraph(expandTarget.nodeId, expandTarget.depth) : fetchGraph(),\n          fetchGraphStats(),\n        ]);\n\n        if (!isActive || !cyRef.current) {\n          return;\n        }\n\n        cyRef.current.elements().remove();\n        cyRef.current.add(toElements(graphPayload));\n        cyRef.current.layout({\n          name: \"cose-bilkent\",\n          fit: true,\n          padding: 140,\n          nodeRepulsion: 240000,\n          idealEdgeLength: 180,\n          edgeElasticity: 0.12,\n          nestingFactor: 0.9,\n          gravity: 0.12,\n          tile: false,\n        } as cytoscape.LayoutOptions).run();\n\n        setStats(graphStats);\n      } catch (loadError) {\n        setError(loadError instanceof Error ? loadError.message : \"Unable to load graph\");\n      } finally {\n        if (isActive) {\n          setLoading(false);\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    void loadGraph();\n\n    return () => {\n      isActive = false;\n    };\n  }, [expandTarget, refreshToken]);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!cyRef.current) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const cy = cyRef.current;\n    cy.elements().removeClass(\"query-highlight active-node active-edge\");\n\n    if (!highlightedNodes.length) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const normalizedIds = new Set(highlightedNodes.map(normalizeHighlightId));\n    const matches = cy.nodes().filter((node) => {\n      const nodeKey = String(node.id());\n      const entityId = String(node.data(\"entity_id\") ?? \"\");\n      return normalizedIds.has(nodeKey) || normalizedIds.has(entityId) || normalizedIds.has(normalizeHighlightId(nodeKey));\n    });\n\n    if (!matches.length) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    matches.addClass(\"query-highlight\");\n  }, [highlightedNodes]);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!cyRef.current || !focusedNodeId) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const cy = cyRef.current;\n    const normalizedTarget = normalizeHighlightId(focusedNodeId);\n    const match = cy.nodes().filter((node) => {\n      const nodeKey = String(node.id());\n      const entityId = String(node.data(\"entity_id\") ?? \"\");\n      return entityId === normalizedTarget || nodeKey === focusedNodeId || normalizeHighlightId(nodeKey) === normalizedTarget;\n    }).first();\n\n    if (!match || match.empty()) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    cy.elements().removeClass(\"active-node active-edge query-highlight\");\n    match.addClass(\"query-highlight\");\n    cy.animate({\n      center: { eles: match },\n      zoom: Math.max(cy.zoom(), 0.9),\n      duration: 350,\n    });\n\n    setOverlayNode(match.data() as CytoscapeNodeData);\n    setOverlayConnections(match.connectedEdges().length);\n    onNodeSelect(buildNodeSummary(match));\n  }, [focusedNodeId, onNodeSelect]);\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"relative flex h-full min-h-0 connections={overlayConnections} node={overlayNode} /> : null}\n\n        {loading ? (\n          <div className=\"absolute inset-0 flex items-center justify-center bg-white/75 backdrop-blur-sm\">\n            <div className=\"rounded-full border border-slate-200 bg-white px-5 py-3 text-sm text-slate-600 shadow-sm\">\n              Loading graph...\n            </div>\n          </div>\n        ) : null}\n\n        {error ? (\n          <div className=\"absolute left-4 top-24 max-w-md rounded-2xl border border-rose-200 bg-rose-50 px-4 py-3 text-sm text-rose-700\">\n            {error}\n          </div>\n        ) : null}\n\n        <div className=\"absolute bottom-4 right-4 rounded-2xl border border-slate-200 bg-white/90 px-4 py-3 text-xs text-slate-500 shadow-sm backdrop-blur\">\n          <div className=\"grid grid-cols-2 gap-x-6 gap-y-2 sm:grid-cols-4\">\n            {Object.entries(NODE_TYPE_COLORS).map(([type, color]) => (\n              <div key={type} className=\"flex items-center gap-2\">\n                <span className=\"size-2 rounded-full\" style={{ backgroundColor: color }} />\n                <span className=\"capitalize\">{type}</span>\n              </div>\n            ))}\n          </div>\n          <div className=\"mt-3 text-[11px] text-slate-400\">{stats ? `${stats.total_nodes} nodes · ${stats.total_edges} edges` : \"Loading stats...\"}</div>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n'@ | Set-Content frontend\\src\\components\\GraphViewer.tsx",

### Assistant
Implemented the chat-to-graph focus behavior you asked for.

What changed:
- when a question is asked, the chat now picks a primary result node
- the graph automatically centers on that node
- only that node is highlighted
- the rest of the graph stays visible, not dimmed
- a floating info card appears on the graph with that node’s details and connection count

- [App.tsx](/c:/Projects/DodgeAI-Assignment/frontend/src/App.tsx)
- [ChatPanel.tsx](/c:/Projects/DodgeAI-Assignment/frontend/src/components/ChatPanel.tsx)
- [GraphViewer.tsx](/c:/Projects/DodgeAI-Assignment/frontend/src/components/GraphViewer.tsx)

### Assistant
I found one clear behavior bug already: the chat focus is picking the wrong “primary” node from the result set, so a question about invoice `90504204` can jump to a payment node instead. I’m fixing that selection logic first, then I’ll make the graph focus use a safer `fit` animation so it recenters cleanly without the visual break you’re seeing.

### Assistant
I’m making two concrete fixes now: the chat will focus the node actually mentioned in the question when possible, and the graph will use a safer fit-to-node animation with generous padding so it stays visible instead of snapping into a broken-looking view.

### Tool Call
shell_command
```json
{
  "command": "$path = 'frontend\\src\\components\\ChatPanel.tsx'; $text = Get-Content $path -Raw; $old = @'\nfunction pickPrimaryNodeId(rawData: unknown, relevantNodeIds: string[] | undefined): string | null {\n  const records =\n    rawData && typeof rawData === \"object\" && \"records\" in rawData && Array.isArray((rawData as { records?: unknown[] }).records)\n      ? (rawData as { records: unknown[] }).records\n      : [];\n\n  const firstRecord = records[0];\n  if (firstRecord && typeof firstRecord === \"object\") {\n    const candidate = firstRecord as Record<string, unknown>;\n    if (typeof candidate.node_id === \"string\") {\n      return candidate.node_id;\n    }\n    if (typeof candidate.entity_id === \"string\") {\n      return candidate.entity_id;\n    }\n    if (Array.isArray(candidate.chain) && candidate.chain.length > 0) {\n      const firstChainNode = candidate.chain[0];\n      if (firstChainNode && typeof firstChainNode === \"object\") {\n        const chainCandidate = firstChainNode as Record<string, unknown>;\n        if (typeof chainCandidate.node_id === \"string\") {\n          return chainCandidate.node_id;\n        }\n        if (typeof chainCandidate.entity_id === \"string\") {\n          return chainCandidate.entity_id;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  const firstRelevant = relevantNodeIds?.find((value) => Boolean(value));\n  return firstRelevant ?? null;\n}\n'@; $new = @'\nfunction pickPrimaryNodeId(question: string, rawData: unknown, relevantNodeIds: string[] | undefined): string | null {\n  const normalizedRelevant = (relevantNodeIds ?? []).filter(Boolean);\n  const idsInQuestion = question.match(/[A-Za-z]?\\d{6,10}/g) ?? [];\n\n  for (const candidate of idsInQuestion) {\n    const exactMatch = normalizedRelevant.find((value) => value === candidate || value.endsWith(`:${candidate}`));\n    if (exactMatch) {\n      return exactMatch;\n    }\n  }\n\n  const records =\n    rawData && typeof rawData === \"object\" && \"records\" in rawData && Array.isArray((rawData as { records?: unknown[] }).records)\n      ? (rawData as { records: unknown[] }).records\n      : [];\n\n  const firstRecord = records[0];\n  if (firstRecord && typeof firstRecord === \"object\") {\n    const candidate = firstRecord as Record<string, unknown>;\n    if (typeof candidate.entity_id === \"string\") {\n      return candidate.entity_id;\n    }\n    if (typeof candidate.node_id === \"string\") {\n      return candidate.node_id;\n    }\n    if (Array.isArray(candidate.chain) && candidate.chain.length > 0) {\n      const matchingChainNode = candidate.chain.find((item) => {\n        if (!item || typeof item !== \"object\") {\n          return false;\n        }\n        const chainCandidate = item as Record<string, unknown>;\n        const entityId = chainCandidate.entity_id;\n        return typeof entityId === \"string\" && idsInQuestion.includes(entityId);\n      });\n      if (matchingChainNode && typeof matchingChainNode === \"object\") {\n        const chainCandidate = matchingChainNode as Record<string, unknown>;\n        if (typeof chainCandidate.entity_id === \"string\") {\n          return chainCandidate.entity_id;\n        }\n        if (typeof chainCandidate.node_id === \"string\") {\n          return chainCandidate.node_id;\n        }\n      }\n      const firstChainNode = candidate.chain[0];\n      if (firstChainNode && typeof firstChainNode === \"object\") {\n        const chainCandidate = firstChainNode as Record<string, unknown>;\n        if (typeof chainCandidate.entity_id === \"string\") {\n          return chainCandidate.entity_id;\n        }\n        if (typeof chainCandidate.node_id === \"string\") {\n          return chainCandidate.node_id;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  return normalizedRelevant[0] ?? null;\n}\n'@; $text = $text.Replace($old, $new); $text = $text.Replace('      const primaryNodeId = pickPrimaryNodeId(response.raw_data, response.relevant_node_ids);', '      const primaryNodeId = pickPrimaryNodeId(question, response.raw_data, response.relevant_node_ids);'); $text = $text.Replace('                  Focused graph on {message.relevantNodeIds[0]}.', '                  Focused graph on the matched result node.'); Set-Content $path $text",

### Assistant
I fixed the query-to-graph focus issue.

What was wrong:
- the chat was choosing the first result node from the response, not the node actually mentioned in the question
- the graph focus animation was centering too aggressively, which could make the view feel visually broken

What I changed:
- chat now prefers the ID mentioned in the question itself
- for `Trace invoice 90504204 flow`, it should now focus `90504204` instead of jumping to `9400000205`
- the graph now uses a padded fit animation, so the focused node stays visible with surrounding context
- only the focused node is highlighted; the rest of the graph stays visible normally
- the floating info card remains open for that focused node

Updated files:
- [ChatPanel.tsx](/c:/Projects/DodgeAI-Assignment/frontend/src/components/ChatPanel.tsx)
- [GraphViewer.tsx](/c:/Projects/DodgeAI-Assignment/frontend/src/components/GraphViewer.tsx)

Verification:
- `npm run build` passed successfully

Please restart the frontend:
```powershell
cd C:\Projects\DodgeAI-Assignment\frontend
npm run dev
```

Then try again:
```text
Trace invoice 90504204 flow
```

Expected now:
- graph stays visible
- node `90504204` is the one focused
- only that node is highlighted
- info card shows for that focused node

If the focused card should appear next to the node instead of top-center, I can do that next.

### Assistant
I found the real UI bug.

The graph was going blank because the Cytoscape instance was being destroyed and recreated on chat focus updates, but the data-loading effect wasn’t rerunning at the same time. So you ended up with a fresh empty canvas after the query.

I’m stabilizing that callback path now so focus/highlight changes won’t rebuild the graph instance.

### Assistant
Found and fixed the blank-graph bug.

Root cause:
- `GraphViewer` was recreating the Cytoscape instance when chat focus/highlight state changed
- that happened because `onClearHighlights` was being passed as a new inline function on every render
- the graph instance got destroyed, but the graph-data load effect did not rerun
- result: empty white canvas after a query

Fix applied:
- stabilized the callback in [App.tsx](/c:/Projects/DodgeAI-Assignment/frontend/src/App.tsx) using `useCallback`
- `GraphViewer` now keeps the existing graph instance alive during chat focus updates

I had already also fixed:
- query focus choosing the wrong node
- safer focus behavior around the matched node

Build status:
- `npm run build` passed

Please restart the frontend:
```powershell
cd C:\Projects\DodgeAI-Assignment\frontend
npm run dev
```

Then retry:
```text
Trace invoice 90504204 flow
```

Expected now:
- graph remains visible
- invoice `90504204` should be the focus target if it’s the queried ID
- only that node gets highlighted
- floating info card shows for the focused node

If this still happens after restart, I’ll do the next debugging step by instrumenting the frontend with a tiny visible runtime status panel for:
- Cytoscape node count
- current zoom
- current focused node
- whether the graph instance was recreated

### User
You are a senior QA engineer and code auditor. You have been given the
complete requirements for a Graph-Based Data Modeling and Query System.

Your ONLY job right now is to AUDIT the entire codebase and verify
that every requirement is met. Do NOT write new code. Just CHECK
and REPORT.

Go through every file in the project and verify against the checklist
below. For each item, respond with:
  ✅ DONE — if fully implemented and working
  ⚠️ PARTIAL — if started but incomplete (explain what's missing)
  ❌ MISSING — if not implemented at all

---

## SECTION 1: DATASET & DATA HANDLING

[ ] All files in C:\Projects\DodgeAI-Assignment\sap-o2c-data are being
    read (list every file you find and confirm it's loaded)
[ ] CSV, JSON, JSONL formats are all handled
[ ] Data is normalized into unified models/schemas
[ ] Entities covered:
    [ ] Orders / Sales Orders
    [ ] Deliveries
    [ ] Invoices / Billing Documents
    [ ] Payments / Journal Entries
    [ ] Customers
    [ ] Products / Materials
    [ ] Addresses / Plants
[ ] Primary keys and foreign keys are identified and mapped
[ ] Preprocessing/deduplication is handled

---

## SECTION 2: GRAPH CONSTRUCTION

[ ] Graph is built using NetworkX (or equivalent)
[ ] NODES exist for each entity type — list the node types created
    and count how many nodes of each type exist
[ ] EDGES exist for relationships — list all edge types created:
    [ ] Purchase Order → Purchase Order Item
    [ ] Delivery → Plant
    [ ] Purchase Order Item → Material
    [ ] Customer → Delivery
    [ ] Order → Delivery
    [ ] Delivery → Invoice/Billing
    [ ] Invoice → Payment/Journal Entry
    [ ] Order → Customer
    [ ] Order → Product
    (Check which of these are implemented, which are missing)
[ ] Each node stores metadata (not just an ID)
[ ] Graph can be exported as JSON

---

## SECTION 3: GRAPH VISUALIZATION (Frontend)

[ ] UI framework is set up (React + Vite + TypeScript)
[ ] Graph visualization library is integrated (Cytoscape.js or alternative)
[ ] Graph renders with nodes and edges visible
[ ] Nodes are color-coded or distinguishable by entity type
[ ] EXPANDING NODES — can you click a node and expand its relationships
[ ] INSPECTING NODE METADATA — clicking a node shows its full data
[ ] VIEWING RELATIONSHIPS — edges are visible and labeled/typed
[ ] Zoom, pan, drag supported
[ ] Layout algorithm applied (not just random positions)
[ ] Clean and usable (doesn't need to be pretty, but must be functional)

---

## SECTION 4: CONVERSATIONAL QUERY INTERFACE

[ ] Chat panel exists in the UI
[ ] User can type a natural language question
[ ] Question is sent to backend POST /api/query
[ ] Backend processes the question through the query pipeline
[ ] LLM is called using Gemini API
[ ] Model used is specifically "gemini-2.0-flash-lite" —
    CHECK the actual model string in the code, confirm it
[ ] If no API key, mock/fallback mode works
[ ] Response contains:
    [ ] Human-readable answer text
    [ ] Relevant node IDs (for highlighting)
    [ ] Raw data
[ ] Answer is displayed in the chat UI
[ ] Relevant nodes are highlighted in the graph when answer returns
[ ] Chat history is maintained during the session

---

## SECTION 5: QUERY CAPABILITIES — TEST THESE SPECIFIC QUERIES

Do NOT just check if the code exists. Actually trace through the logic
and confirm whether the system COULD answer these:

[ ] "Which products are associated with the highest number of billing documents"
    → Does the system support aggregation/counting across relationships
    → Is there a path from Product → Billing Document in the graph

[ ] "Trace the full flow of a given billing document
    (Sales Order → Delivery → Billing → Journal Entry)"
    → Does the system support chain/path traversal
    → Are all 4 entity types (Sales Order, Delivery, Billing,
      Journal Entry) present as nodes
    → Are edges connecting them in sequence

[ ] "Identify sales orders that have broken or incomplete flows
    (e.g. delivered but not billed, billed without delivery)"
    → Does the system support finding nodes with MISSING expected
      relationships
    → Can it detect: Order has Delivery but no Invoice
    → Can it detect: Invoice exists but no Delivery linked

For each query, explain:
  - Can the current graph structure support this query (Yes/No/Partially)
  - Can the query pipeline translate this (Yes/No/Partially)
  - What would break or is missing

---

## SECTION 6: GUARDRAILS

[ ] Guardrail check exists BEFORE LLM calls
[ ] Test these inputs mentally — would they be rejected
    [ ] "What is the capital of France" → MUST be rejected
    [ ] "Write me a poem" → MUST be rejected
    [ ] "How do I cook pasta" → MUST be rejected
    [ ] "Tell me about machine learning" → MUST be rejected
[ ] Rejection response is exactly or similar to:
    "This system is designed to answer questions related to the
    provided dataset only."
[ ] Guardrail does NOT reject valid dataset questions like:
    [ ] "Show me all orders" → MUST pass
    [ ] "What deliveries are linked to order X" → MUST pass
    [ ] "Total payment amount" → MUST pass

---

## SECTION 7: LLM PROMPTING STRATEGY

[ ] Every LLM call uses structured prompting with:
    [ ] ROLE defined
    [ ] SCHEMA CONTEXT injected (actual node types, edge types, fields)
    [ ] TASK clearly stated
    [ ] OUTPUT FORMAT enforced (strict JSON for translation)
[ ] Schema context is dynamically generated from actual data models
    (not hardcoded strings that could go stale)
[ ] The translation prompt forces JSON output, not free text

---

## SECTION 8: LOGGING

[ ] File exists: backend/logs/llm_sessions.md
[ ] Every query appends to this file
[ ] Each log entry contains:
    [ ] Timestamp
    [ ] User question
    [ ] Guardrail result
    [ ] Full prompt sent to LLM (translation step)
    [ ] LLM response (translation step)
    [ ] Parsed structured query
    [ ] Execution result summary
    [ ] Full prompt sent to LLM (answer generation step)
    [ ] LLM response (answer generation step)
    [ ] Final answer returned to user
[ ] Logs demonstrate iteration/debugging capability

---

## SECTION 9: API ENDPOINTS

[ ] GET  /api/graph — returns full graph JSON
[ ] GET  /api/graph/stats — returns node/edge counts
[ ] GET  /api/node/{id} — returns node metadata + neighbors
[ ] POST /api/graph/node — adds a node
[ ] POST /api/graph/edge — adds an edge
[ ] DELETE /api/graph/reset — rebuilds graph from data
[ ] GET  /api/graph/export — returns downloadable JSON
[ ] POST /api/query — accepts { question } returns { answer, node_ids, raw_data }
[ ] CORS enabled for frontend

---

## SECTION 10: TOOLBAR (Frontend)

[ ] Add Node button + form
[ ] Add Edge button + form
[ ] Reset Graph button
[ ] Export JSON button (downloads file)

---

## SECTION 11: PROJECT HYGIENE

[ ] .env.example exists with GEMINI_API_KEY placeholder
[ ] .env is in .gitignore
[ ] requirements.txt has all Python dependencies
[ ] package.json has all frontend dependencies
[ ] README.md exists with:
    [ ] Setup instructions
    [ ] How to configure API key
    [ ] Sample queries
    [ ] Architecture overview

---

## YOUR OUTPUT FORMAT

Give me a summary table:

| # | Requirement | Status | Notes |
|---|-------------|--------|-------|
| 1 | Dataset loading | ✅/⚠️/❌ | ... |
| 2 | ... | ... | ... |

Then a section: "CRITICAL GAPS" — list anything that would FAIL
the assignment evaluation.

Then a section: "RECOMMENDED FIXES" — ordered by priority,
what to fix first.

Do NOT fix anything yourself. Just audit and report.

from __future__ import annotations

from collections import Counter
from typing import Any

import networkx as nx

from .data_loader import NormalizedData

class GraphBuilder:
    def __init__(self, data: NormalizedData) -> None:
        self.data = data
        self.graph = nx.MultiDiGraph()
        self._build()

    @staticmethod
    def _node_key(entity_type: str, entity_id: str) -> str:
        return f"{entity_type}:{entity_id}"

    def _build(self) -> None:
        for customer in self.data.customers:
            self._add_entity_node("customer", customer)

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path
from threading import Lock

from .data_loader import DEFAULT_DATA_DIR, DataLoader, NormalizedData
from .graph_builder import GraphBuilder

class GraphStore:
    _instance: "GraphStore | None" = None
    _instance_lock = Lock()

    def __new__(cls) -> "GraphStore":
        with cls._instance_lock:
            if cls._instance is None:
                cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)
                cls._instance._initialized = False
                cls._instance._graph_builder = None
                cls._instance._data = None
                cls._instance._data_dir = None
                cls._instance._lock = Lock()
        return cls._instance

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any

from fastapi import APIRouter
from pydantic import BaseModel

from backend.agents.query_pipeline import QueryPipeline
from backend.services.graph_store import graph_store

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api", tags=["query"])

class QueryRequest(BaseModel):
    question: str

@router.post("/query")
async def query_graph(request: QueryRequest) -> dict[str, Any]:
    pipeline = QueryPipeline(graph_store.graph)
    return await pipeline.run(request.question)
```

from __future__ import annotations

from backend.services.data_loader import DEFAULT_DATA_DIR, DataLoader
from backend.services.graph_builder import GraphBuilder
from backend.services.graph_store import graph_store

def _find_full_chain(builder: GraphBuilder) -> tuple[str, str, str, str] | None:
    graph = builder.graph
    for node_key, attrs in graph.nodes(data=True):
        if attrs.get("entity_type") != "order":
            continue

        for delivery_key in graph.successors(node_key):
            if graph.nodes[delivery_key].get("entity_type") != "delivery":
                continue
            for invoice_key in graph.successors(delivery_key):
                if graph.nodes[invoice_key].get("entity_type") != "invoice":
                    continue
                for payment_key in graph.successors(invoice_key):
                    if graph.nodes[payment_key].get("entity_type") == "payment":
                        return (
                            graph.nodes[node_key]["entity_id"],
... [truncated 27 additional lines]

Output:
# SAP O2C Graph Query System

A full-stack graph exploration system for SAP Order-to-Cash data. The backend ingests raw O2C datasets, normalizes business entities, builds an in-memory NetworkX graph, and exposes graph/query APIs through FastAPI. The frontend renders the graph with Cytoscape.js and adds a natural-language chat workflow for guided exploration.

## What This Project Does

- Loads SAP O2C datasets from `sap-o2c-data`
- Normalizes Orders, Deliveries, Invoices, Payments, Customers, Products, and Addresses
- Builds a typed relationship graph in NetworkX
- Exposes graph inspection and mutation APIs with FastAPI
- Supports natural-language querying through a unified LLM query pipeline
- Visualizes nodes, edges, metadata, and query highlights in React + Cytoscape.js

## Dataset Scope

The current dataset covers the order-to-cash chain:

`Customer -> Order -> Delivery -> Invoice -> Payment`

Supported graph node types:

- `Order`
... [truncated 187 additional lines]
```

### Assistant
| # | Requirement | Status | Notes |
|---|-------------|--------|-------|
| 1 | Dataset loading | ✅ DONE | `backend/services/data_loader.py` recursively reads all supported files under `sap-o2c-data`. I found 49 files, all `.jsonl`, and the loader is written to iterate them all by suffix. |
| 2 | CSV / JSON / JSONL handling | ⚠️ PARTIAL | Code supports `.csv`, `.json`, `.jsonl`, but the actual dataset in this repo contains only `.jsonl`, so CSV/JSON support is implemented but not evidenced by real project data. |
| 3 | Unified normalization / schemas | ✅ DONE | Raw records are normalized into Pydantic models: `CustomerRecord`, `AddressRecord`, `ProductRecord`, `OrderRecord`, `DeliveryRecord`, `InvoiceRecord`, `PaymentRecord`, wrapped by `NormalizedData`. |
| 4 | Entity coverage | ⚠️ PARTIAL | Orders, Deliveries, Invoices, Payments, Customers, Products, Addresses are normalized. `plants` data exists on disk but is not normalized into its own model or graph node type. |
| 5 | PK/FK mapping | ✅ DONE | IDs and relationships are mapped in loader logic: order-customer, order-product, order-delivery, delivery-invoice, invoice-payment, customer-address. |
| 6 | Preprocessing / deduplication | ✅ DONE | Cleaning, null normalization, ID normalization, numeric/date coercion, merge logic, and dedup-by-key maps are implemented. |
| 7 | Graph engine | ✅ DONE | Graph is built with `networkx.MultiDiGraph` in `backend/services/graph_builder.py`. |
| 8 | Graph node types and counts | ✅ DONE | Current in-memory graph stats: `address=8`, `customer=8`, `delivery=86`, `invoice=163`, `order=100`, `payment=120`, `product=69`; total `554` nodes. |
| 9 | Required edge coverage | ⚠️ PARTIAL | Implemented: `Order→Delivery`, `Delivery→Invoice`, `Invoice→Payment`, `Order→Customer`, `Order→Product`, `Customer→Address`. Missing: `Purchase Order→Purchase Order Item`, `Delivery→Plant`, `Purchase Order Item→Material`, `Customer→Delivery`. Total edges: `732`. |
| 10 | Node metadata + graph export | ✅ DONE | Nodes store full metadata attributes, not just IDs. Graph export to Cytoscape-style JSON is implemented. |
| 11 | Frontend stack / graph rendering | ✅ DONE | React + Vite + TypeScript are set up. Cytoscape is integrated. Graph renders with visible nodes/edges, layout algorithm, zoom/pan/drag. |
| 12 | Frontend inspection / expansion / relationship display | ⚠️ PARTIAL | Clicking a node shows metadata. Node expansion exists via `NodeDetail` “Expand” subgraph action. Edge lines are visible, but edge labels/types are not shown on the graph. |
| 13 | Chat UI + backend query flow | ✅ DONE | Chat panel exists, accepts natural language input, calls `POST /api/query`, backend runs `QueryPipeline`, and answer text is displayed with session history. |
| 14 | Gemini integration | ⚠️ PARTIAL | Gemini is integrated and callable via `google-genai`, but the code uses model string `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview`, not the explicitly requested `gemini-2.0-flash-lite`. |
| 15 | Fallback / mock mode | ✅ DONE | If `GEMINI_API_KEY` is missing or Gemini import fails, the pipeline falls back to rule-based mock translation/answer generation. |
| 16 | Query response shape / graph highlighting | ⚠️ PARTIAL | Backend returns `{ answer, relevant_node_ids, raw_data }` and frontend uses it. However, the checklist expected `node_ids`, and the UI currently focuses a primary node rather than consistently highlighting all returned nodes. |
| 17 | Query capability: “Which products are associated with the highest number of billing documents” | ⚠️ PARTIAL | Graph can partially support this because products carry invoice references in metadata, and pipeline has aggregate/count handlers. But there is no explicit `Product→Invoice/Billing` edge, so this is metadata-based rather than graph-relationship-based. |
| 18 | Query capability: “Trace full flow of billing document (Sales Order → Delivery → Billing → Journal Entry)” | ⚠️ PARTIAL | Sales Order, Delivery, Billing/Invoice, and Payment exist. Journal Entry does not exist as its own node type. Chain traversal supports order→delivery→invoice→payment, not order→delivery→billing→journal-entry. |
| 19 | Query capability: broken / incomplete flows | ❌ MISSING | There is no execution path for anomaly queries such as “delivered but not billed” or “billed without delivery.” The graph could be analyzed for this, but the current query pipeline does not implement missing-relationship detection. |
| 20 | Guardrails | ✅ DONE | Guardrail check runs before LLM calls. Off-domain prompts like “What is the capital of France”, “Write me a poem”, “How do I cook pasta”, and “Tell me about machine learning” would be rejected; valid dataset questions would pass. |
| 21 | Prompting strategy | ✅ DONE | LLM prompts use structured prompting with role, dynamic schema context, explicit task, and strict JSON output for translation. Schema context is generated from the actual graph/models, not a static hardcoded string. |
| 22 | LLM logging | ⚠️ PARTIAL | `backend/logs/llm_sessions.md` exists and is appended to. It contains timestamped stage logs with prompts and responses, but it does not consistently record all required fields as separate entries: execution summary, parsed structured query as a clearly named field, and final answer returned. |
| 23 | API endpoints | ⚠️ PARTIAL | All listed endpoints exist and are wired, and CORS is enabled for `localhost:5173`. Minor mismatch: `/api/query` returns `relevant_node_ids` instead of the checklist’s `node_ids`. |
| 24 | Toolbar actions | ✅ DONE | Add Node, Add Edge, Reset Graph, and Export JSON are implemented in the frontend and wired to backend endpoints. |
| 25 | Project hygiene | ✅ DONE | `.env.example` exists with `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `.env` is ignored, Python and frontend dependency manifests exist, and `README.md` includes setup, API key config, sample questions, and architecture overview. |

**CRITICAL GAPS**
- The Gemini model does not match the stated requirement. Code uses `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview`, not `gemini-2.0-flash-lite`.
- Journal Entry is not modeled as its own graph node type, so the required billing-document-to-journal-entry flow is not fully represented.
- Plant data exists in the dataset but is not normalized into nodes or linked via `Delivery→Plant`.
- Several required relationships are missing from the graph entirely: `Purchase Order→Purchase Order Item`, `Purchase Order Item→Material`, `Customer→Delivery`, `Delivery→Plant`.
- The system cannot currently answer “broken/incomplete flow” questions because missing-relationship anomaly detection is not implemented in the execution layer.
- Logging is not complete enough for audit/debug standards in the checklist; it does not consistently capture execution summary and final returned answer as explicit structured stages.
- Frontend does not display edge types/labels, so relationship semantics are not visually inspectable in the graph itself.

**RECOMMENDED FIXES**
1. Add missing graph entities and edges first: `JournalEntry`, `Plant`, and the missing relationship types. This is the biggest modeling gap.
2. Extend the query executor to support anomaly/missing-link analysis so “delivered not billed” and “billed without delivery” questions are answerable.
3. Align the Gemini model string with the exact required model, or explicitly update the requirement if the new model is intentional.
4. Promote billing/product and delivery/plant relationships from metadata-only references into explicit graph edges.
5. Make `/api/query` response contract match the required shape exactly, or document the intentional deviation.
6. Upgrade `llm_sessions.md` logging to include explicit fields/stages for guardrail result, parsed structured query, execution summary, answer prompt, answer response, and final returned answer.
7. Add edge labels or a relationship inspector in the frontend so users can see relationship types, not just lines.
8. Validate CSV and JSON ingestion with at least one real sample each, since current repo data only demonstrates JSONL.

### User
You are a senior full-stack engineer working on an existing Graph-Based
Data Modeling and Query System. The project already has:
- Python FastAPI backend with NetworkX graph
- Gemini LLM query pipeline (model: gemini-2.0-flash-lite)
- React + Vite + TypeScript frontend with Cytoscape.js graph viewer
- Chat panel for natural language queries
- Guardrails for off-topic rejection

Your job now is to add BONUS FEATURES. I want DEPTH over breadth.
Focus on making each feature SOLID and COMPLETE, not half-done.

Dataset path: C:\Projects\DodgeAI-Assignment\sap-o2c-data

---

## FEATURE 1: TRANSPARENT QUERY TRANSLATION (DEEP IMPLEMENTATION)
Priority: HIGHEST — This is a core differentiator.

Right now the LLM translates natural language → structured JSON internally
and the user never sees it. CHANGE THAT.

### Backend Changes:

1. Update the query pipeline response to include the generated
   structured query visibly:
   {
     "answer": "string",
     "relevant_node_ids": [],
     "raw_data": {},
     "generated_query": {
       "type": "graph_traversal" | "sql",
       "query_string": "the actual query as readable text",
       "structured_form": { the JSON operation object },
       "explanation": "1-2 sentence explanation of what this query does"
     },
     "execution_time_ms": 142
   }

2. Add a DUAL QUERY MODE in the pipeline:

   MODE A — Graph Traversal (existing):
   Translate NL → structured JSON → execute on NetworkX

   MODE B — SQL Translation (NEW):
   - On startup, also load the normalized data into an in-memory
     SQLite database (one table per entity type)
   - Define the SQL schema clearly
   - When a query comes in, generate BOTH:
     a) A graph traversal operation
     b) An equivalent SQL query string
   - Execute via SQL using sqlite3
   - Return BOTH the graph traversal result AND the SQL query string

   The SQL must be VISIBLE to the user in the UI. This proves the
   system is doing real translation, not faking it.

3. Update the Gemini prompt for translation to output:
   {
     "graph_operation": { ...existing structured query... },
     "sql_query": "SELECT o.order_id, d.delivery_id FROM orders o JOIN deliveries d ON o.order_id = d.order_id WHERE ...",
     "explanation": "This finds all deliveries linked to orders matching the given criteria"
   }

4. Create backend/services/sql_engine.py:
   - On startup, create SQLite tables from normalized data
   - Tables: orders, deliveries, invoices, payments, customers,
     products, addresses (match your entity schemas)
   - Function: execute_sql(query_string) → results as list of dicts
   - SAFETY: Validate generated SQL before execution
     - Only allow SELECT statements
     - Block DROP, DELETE, UPDATE, INSERT, ALTER
     - Parameterize where possible
     - Wrap in try/except, return clean error on bad SQL

### Frontend Changes:

5. In the ChatPanel, when a response comes back, show an EXPANDABLE
   section below the answer:

   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
   │ 🤖 Answer:                              │
   │ There are 15 deliveries linked to       │
   │ customer C001...                         │
   │                                          │
   │ ▶ View Generated Query                  │  ← clickable toggle
   │ ┌───────────────────────────────────┐    │
   │ │ 📊 SQL:                           │    │
   │ │ SELECT d.delivery_id, d.status    │    │
   │ │ FROM deliveries d                 │    │
   │ │ JOIN orders o ON ...              │    │
   │ │ WHERE o.customer_id = 'C001'      │    │
   │ │                                   │    │
   │ │ 🔀 Graph Traversal:              │    │
   │ │ find_node(Customer, id=C001)      │    │
   │ │   → traverse(Order, depth=1)      │    │
   │ │   → traverse(Delivery, depth=1)   │    │
   │ │                                   │    │
   │ │ ⏱️ Executed in 142ms              │    │
   │ └───────────────────────────────────┘    │
   └─────────────────────────────────────────┘

   Use syntax highlighting for the SQL (use a simple <pre> with
   monospace font and keyword coloring, or use a lightweight lib
   like highlight.js — but do NOT add a heavy dependency).

---

## FEATURE 2: STREAMING RESPONSES (DEEP IMPLEMENTATION)
Priority: HIGH — Makes the UX feel alive.

### Backend:

1. Create a NEW endpoint:
   POST /api/query/stream
   - Same input: { "question": "string" }
   - Returns a Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream
   - Stream stages:

     event: status
     data: {"stage": "guardrail", "message": "Checking query relevance..."}

     event: status
     data: {"stage": "translating", "message": "Translating to structured query..."}

     event: query
     data: {"generated_query": { sql, graph_op, explanation }}

     event: status
     data: {"stage": "executing", "message": "Running query on graph..."}

     event: status
     data: {"stage": "generating", "message": "Generating answer..."}

     event: token
     data: {"text": "There"}

     event: token
     data: {"text": " are"}

     event: token
     data: {"text": " 15"}
     ... (stream answer token by token)

     event: complete
     data: {"answer": "full answer", "relevant_node_ids": [...], "raw_data": {...}}

2. Use FastAPI's StreamingResponse with text/event-stream content type

3. For the Gemini answer generation step, use streaming mode:
   - google-genai supports streaming via generate_content with stream=True
   - Yield each chunk as an SSE token event

4. Keep the original POST /api/query as non-streaming fallback

### Frontend:

5. Update ChatPanel to use EventSource or fetch with ReadableStream:
   - Connect to /api/query/stream
   - Show a PROGRESS INDICATOR with the current stage:
     "🔍 Checking query..." → "🔄 Translating..." → "⚡ Executing..."
     → "💬 Generating answer..."
   - Stream the answer text character by character as it arrives
   - When complete event arrives, finalize the message and trigger
     node highlighting on the graph

6. Show a typing indicator animation while tokens stream in

7. If streaming fails, fall back to the regular /api/query endpoint

---

## FEATURE 3: CONVERSATION MEMORY (DEEP IMPLEMENTATION)
Priority: HIGH — Makes multi-turn queries actually work.

### Backend:

1. Create backend/agents/memory.py:

   class ConversationMemory:
       - Stores the last N turns (default N=10) per session
       - Each turn: { role: "user"|"assistant", content: str,
         timestamp: str, query_generated: dict|None }
       - Methods:
         - add_turn(role, content, query=None)
         - get_context() → formatted string of conversation history
         - get_last_entities() → extract entity IDs/types mentioned
           in recent turns
         - clear()
         - to_dict() → serializable

2. Store sessions in memory using a dict keyed by session_id:
   sessions: dict[str, ConversationMemory] = {}

3. Update POST /api/query and /api/query/stream:
   - Accept optional header or body field: session_id
   - If no session_id, generate one and return it
   - Load conversation memory for that session
   - INJECT conversation history into the Gemini translation prompt:

     """
     CONVERSATION HISTORY:
     User: "Show me all orders for customer C001"
     Assistant: Found 5 orders: O_1001, O_1002, O_1003, O_1004, O_1005

     User: "Which of these have deliveries"     ← THIS is the new query
     """

     The LLM can now resolve "these" → the 5 orders from previous turn

   - After response, add both user question and answer to memory

4. Add endpoint:
   DELETE /api/session/{session_id}
   - Clears conversation memory for that session

5. Handle PRONOUNS and REFERENCES:
   - "Show me orders for customer C001" → works
   - "Which of these were delivered" → "these" = previous results
   - "What about their invoices" → "their" = the deliveries from above
   - "Go deeper on the first one" → first result from previous query

   The conversation history in the prompt handles this naturally —
   the LLM sees prior context and resolves references.

### Frontend:

6. Generate a session_id (uuid) when the app loads, store in state
7. Send session_id with every query
8. Add a "New Conversation" button that:
   - Calls DELETE /api/session/{session_id}
   - Generates new session_id
   - Clears chat history in UI
9. Show a subtle indicator: "Memory: 3/10 turns"

---

## FEATURE 4: NODE HIGHLIGHTING (POLISH EXISTING)
Priority: MEDIUM — Already partially there, make it solid.

### Frontend — GraphViewer updates:

1. When a query response includes relevant_node_ids:
   - Animate those nodes: pulse effect (scale up/down with CSS animation)
   - Change border color to bright yellow/gold
   - Increase border width
   - Bring them to front (z-index)
   - Auto-zoom the viewport to fit all highlighted nodes
     (use cy.fit(highlightedNodes, padding))

2. If the query was a TRAVERSAL/CHAIN:
   - Highlight the PATH edges too, not just nodes
   - Color the path edges in a different color (e.g., bright cyan)
   - Animate the edges with a dashed moving pattern if possible

3. CLICKING a highlighted node in the graph should scroll the chat
   panel to the message that referenced it

4. Highlights auto-clear after 15 seconds OR when user clicks
   empty space OR when a new query is submitted

5. Add a small badge on highlighted nodes showing WHY they were
   highlighted (e.g., "Matched: order_id=O_1001")

---

## FEATURE 5: GRAPH CLUSTERING (LIGHTER IMPLEMENTATION)
Priority: LOWER — But adds analytical depth.

### Backend:

1. Create backend/services/graph_analysis.py:

   Functions:
   - get_clusters() → Run community detection on the NetworkX graph
     Use: networkx.algorithms.community (Louvain or label propagation)
     Return: list of clusters with their node IDs and sizes

   - get_node_importance() → Run PageRank or degree centrality
     Return: dict of node_id → importance_score
     Identify the most "connected" entities

   - get_broken_flows() → Find orders with incomplete chains
     An order SHOULD have: Order → Delivery → Invoice → Payment
     Find orders where any step is missing
     Return: {
       "complete": [list of order_ids],
       "missing_delivery": [list],
       "missing_invoice": [list],
       "missing_payment": [list]
     }

2. Add endpoints:
   GET /api/graph/clusters → returns cluster data
   GET /api/graph/importance → returns top N important nodes
   GET /api/graph/broken-flows → returns incomplete chains

### Frontend:

3. Add a "Analytics" tab or section in the toolbar:
   - Button: "Show Clusters" → color nodes by cluster
   - Button: "Show Important Nodes" → scale node size by importance
   - Button: "Find Broken Flows" → highlight incomplete chains in red
   - Button: "Reset View" → back to normal coloring/sizing

4. When clusters are shown, add a legend showing cluster colors

from fastapi import APIRouter

from backend.agents.query_pipeline import QueryPipeline

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api", tags=["query"])

    question: str

@router.post("/query")
async def query_graph(request: QueryRequest) -> dict[str, Any]:

optional dependency in mock mode\n    genai = None\n    types = None\n\n\ndef _load_env() -> None:\n    if ENV_PATH.exists():\n        load_dotenv(ENV_PATH, override=True)\n\n\ndef get_gemini_api_key() -> str:\n    _load_env()\n    return os.getenv(\"GEMINI_API_KEY\", \"\").strip()\n\n\ndef is_gemini_available() -> bool:\n    return bool(get_gemini_api_key()) and genai is not None and types is not None\n\n\nasync def call_gemini(prompt: str, system_instruction: str) -> str:\n    api_key = get_gemini_api_key()\n    if not api_key:\n        LOGGER.warning(\"No Gemini API key found, using mock mode\")\n        raise RuntimeError(\"No Gemini API key found, using mock mode\")\n    if genai is None or types is None:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"google-genai is not installed\")\n\n    client = genai.Client(api_key=api_key)\n\n    async def _invoke() -> str:\n        def _sync_call() -> str:\n            response = client.models.generate_content(\n                model=MODEL_NAME,\n                contents=prompt,\n                config=types.GenerateContentConfig(\n                    system_instruction=system_instruction,\n                    temperature=0.1,\n                ),\n            )\n            text = getattr(response, \"text\", None)\n            if not text or not str(text).strip():\n                raise RuntimeError(\"Gemini returned an empty response\")\n            return str(text).strip()\n\n        return await asyncio.to_thread(_sync_call)\n\n    last_error: Exception | None = None\n    for attempt in range(2):\n        try:\n            return await asyncio.wait_for(_invoke(), timeout=30)\n        except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - network dependent\n            last_error = exc\n            LOGGER.warning(\"Gemini call attempt %s failed: %s\", attempt + 1, exc)\n            await asyncio.sleep(1 + attempt)\n    raise RuntimeError(f\"Gemini call failed: {last_error}\")\n\n\nasync def stream_gemini(prompt: str, system_instruction: str) -> AsyncIterator[str]:\n    api_key = get_gemini_api_key()\n    if not api_key:\n        LOGGER.warning(\"No Gemini API key found, using mock mode\")\n        raise RuntimeError(\"No Gemini API key found, using mock mode\")\n    if genai is None or types is None:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"google-genai is not installed\")\n\n    client = genai.Client(api_key=api_key)\n    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()\n    queue: asyncio.Queue[str | None] = asyncio.Queue()\n\n    def _emit(value: str | None) -> None:\n        loop.call_soon_threadsafe(queue.put_nowait, value)\n\n    def _worker() -> None:\n        try:\n            stream = client.models.generate_content_stream(\n                model=MODEL_NAME,\n                contents=prompt,\n                config=types.GenerateContentConfig(\n                    system_instruction=system_instruction,\n                    temperature=0.1,\n                ),\n            )\n            for chunk in stream:\n                text = getattr(chunk, \"text\", None)\n                if text:\n                    _emit(str(text))\n        except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - network dependent\n            LOGGER.warning(\"Gemini streaming call failed: %s\", exc)\n        finally:\n            _emit(None)\n\n    worker = asyncio.create_task(asyncio.to_thread(_worker))\n    try:\n        while True:\n            chunk = await asyncio.wait_for(queue.get(), timeout=60)\n            if chunk is None:\n                break\n            if chunk:\n                yield chunk\n    finally:\n        if not worker.done():\n            worker.cancel()\n'@ | Set-Content backend\\agents\\llm_client.py",

Any\n\nENTITY_PATTERNS = {\n    \"Order\": re.compile(r\"\\b74\\d{4}\\b\"),\n    \"Delivery\": re.compile(r\"\\b80\\d{6}\\b\"),\n    \"Invoice\": re.compile(r\"\\b(?:90|91)\\d{6}\\b\"),\n    \"Payment\": re.compile(r\"\\b94\\d{8}\\b\"),\n    \"Customer\": re.compile(r\"\\b32\\d{7}\\b\"),\n}\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass ConversationTurn:\n    role: str\n    content: str\n    timestamp: str\n    query_generated: dict[str, Any] | None = None\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass ConversationMemory:\n    max_turns: int = 10\n    turns: deque[ConversationTurn] = field(default_factory=deque)\n\n    def add_turn(self, role: str, content: str, query: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> None:\n        timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n        self.turns.append(ConversationTurn(role=role, content=content, timestamp=timestamp, query_generated=query))\n        while len(self.turns) > self.max_turns:\n            self.turns.popleft()\n\n    def get_context(self) -> str:\n        if not self.turns:\n            return \"No prior conversation.\"\n        lines: list[str] = []\n        for turn in self.turns:\n            lines.append(f\"{turn.role.capitalize()}: {turn.content}\")\n            if turn.query_generated:\n                lines.append(f\"Generated Query: {turn.query_generated}\")\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n    def get_last_entities(self) -> list[dict[str, str]]:\n        found: list[dict[str, str]] = []\n        seen: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()\n        for turn in reversed(self.turns):\n            for entity_type, pattern in ENTITY_PATTERNS.items():\n                for match in pattern.findall(turn.content):\n                    key = (entity_type, match)\n                    if key in seen:\n                        continue\n                    seen.add(key)\n                    found.append({\"entity_type\": entity_type, \"entity_id\": match})\n        return found\n\n    def clear(self) -> None:\n        self.turns.clear()\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        return {\n            \"max_turns\": self.max_turns,\n            \"turn_count\": len(self.turns),\n            \"turns\": [\n                {\n                    \"role\": turn.role,\n                    \"content\": turn.content,\n                    \"timestamp\": turn.timestamp,\n                    \"query_generated\": turn.query_generated,\n                }\n                for turn in self.turns\n            ],\n            \"last_entities\": self.get_last_entities(),\n        }\n\n\nsessions: dict[str, ConversationMemory] = {}\n\n\ndef get_session_memory(session_id: str, max_turns: int = 10) -> ConversationMemory:\n    memory = sessions.get(session_id)\n    if memory is None:\n        memory = ConversationMemory(max_turns=max_turns)\n        sessions[session_id] = memory\n    return memory\n\n\ndef clear_session_memory(session_id: str) -> None:\n    memory = sessions.pop(session_id, None)\n    if memory is not None:\n        memory.clear()\n'@ | Set-Content backend\\agents\\memory.py",
  "workdir": "c:\\Projects\\DodgeAI-Assignment",
  "timeout_ms": 120000
}

record.metadata,\n                }\n                for record in self.data.payments\n            ],\n        )\n        self._insert_many(\n            \"customer_addresses\",\n            [\n                {\"customer_id\": customer.id, \"address_id\": address_id}\n                for customer in self.data.customers\n                for address_id in customer.address_ids\n            ],\n query_string: str) -> None:\n        query = query_string.strip()\n        if not query:\n            raise ValueError(\"Generated SQL is empty.\")\n        lowered = query.lower()\n        if not (lowered.startswith(\"select\") or lowered.startswith(\"with\")):\n            raise ValueError(\"Only SELECT statements are allowed.\")\n        for pattern in BANNED_SQL_PATTERNS:\n            if pattern.search(query):\n                raise ValueError(\"Unsafe SQL detected.\")\n\n    def execute_sql(self, query_string: str) -> SqlExecutionResult:\n        try:\n            self.validate_sql(query_string)\n            cursor = self.connection.execute(query_string)\n            rows = [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]\n            return SqlExecutionResult(query=query_string, rows=rows)\n        except Exception as exc:\n            return SqlExecutionResult(query=query_string, rows=[], error=str(exc))\n\n    def get_schema_overview(self) -> dict[str, list[str]]:\n        schema: dict[str, list[str]] = {}\n        cursor = self.connection.execute(\"SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' ORDER BY name\")\n        tables = [row[0] for row in cursor.fetchall()]\n        for table in tables:\n            pragma = self.connection.execute(f\"PRAGMA table_info({table})\")\n            schema[table]   "command": "@'\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport networkx as nx\nfrom networkx.algorithms.community import label_propagation_communities\n\nfrom backend.services.graph_builder import GraphBuilder\n\n\nclass GraphAnalysisService:\n    def __init__(self, builder: GraphBuilder) -> None:\n        self.builder = builder\n\n    def get_clusters(self) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n        undirected = self.builder.graph.to_undirected()\n        communities = list(label_propagation_communities(undirected))\n        clusters: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n        for index, community in enumerate(communities, start=1):\n            node_ids = sorted(\n                str(self.builder.graph.nodes[node_key].get(\"entity_id\", node_key))\n                for node_key in community\n            )\n            clusters.append(\n                {\n                    \"cluster_id\": f\"cluster_{index}\",\n                    \"size\": len(node_ids),\n                    \"node_ids\": node_ids,\n                }\n            )\n        return sorted(clusters, key=lambda cluster: cluster[\"size\"], reverse=True)\n\n    def get_node_importance(self, limit: int = 25) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n        scores = nx.pagerank(self.builder.graph.to_undirected())\n        ranked = sorted(scores.items(), key=lambda item: item[1], reverse=True)[:limit]\n        results = []\n        for node_key, score in ranked:\n            attrs = self.builder.graph.nodes[node_key]\n            results.append(\n                {\n                    \"node_key\": node_key,\n                    \"entity_id\": attrs.get(\"entity_id\"),\n                    \"entity_type\": attrs.get(\"entity_type\"),\n                    \"label\": attrs.get(\"label\"),\n                    \"importance_score\": round(score, 6),\n                }\n            )\n        return results\n\n    def get_broken_flows(self) -> dict[str, list[str]]:\n        graph = self.builder.graph\n        result: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)\n        for node_key, attrs in graph.nodes(data=True):\n            if attrs.get(\"entity_type\") != \"order\":\n                continue\n            order_id = str(attrs.get(\"entity_id\"))\n            delivery_keys = [key for key in graph.successors(node_key) if graph.nodes[key].get(\"entity_type\") == \"delivery\"]\n            if not delivery_keys:\n                result[\"missing_delivery\"].append(order_id)\n                continue\n            invoice_keys = []\n            for delivery_key in delivery_keys:\n                invoice_keys.extend(\n                    key for key in graph.successors(delivery_key) if graph.nodes[key].get(\"entity_type\") == \"invoice\"\n                )\n            if not invoice_keys:\n                result[\"missing_invoice\"].append(order_id)\n                continue\n            payment_keys = []\n            for invoice_key in invoice_keys:\n                payment_keys.extend(\n                    key for key in graph.successors(invoice_key) if graph.nodes[key].get(\"entity_type\") == \"payment\"\n                )\n            if not payment_keys:\n                result[\"missing_payment\"].append(order_id)\n                continue\n            result[\"complete\"].append(order_id)\n\n        return {\n            \"complete\": sorted(result.get(\"complete\", [])),\n            \"missing_delivery\": sorted(result.get(\"missing_delivery\", [])),\n            \"missing_invoice\": sorted(result.get(\"missing_invoice\", [])),\n            \"missing_payment\": sorted(result.get(\"missing_payment\", [])),\n        }\n'@ | Set-Content backend\\services\\graph_analysis.py",

\"GraphStore\":\n        with cls._instance_lock:\n            if cls._instance is None:\n                cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)\n                cls._instance._initialized = False\n                cls._instance._graph_builder = None\n                cls._instance._sql_engine = None\n                cls._instance._data = None\n                cls._instance._data_dir = None\n   self._initialized = True\n            return self._graph_builder\n\n    def reset(self) -> GraphBuilder:\n        return self.initialize(force=True)\n\n    @property\n    def graph(self) -> question)\n            memory.add_turn(\"assistant\", answer, query=translation.model_dump(mode=\"json\"))\n\n        self._append_log(\n            stage=\"complete\",\n yield \"complete\", {\n            \"answer\": answer,\n            \"relevant_node_ids\": relevant_node_ids,\n            \"raw_data\": raw_data,\n            \"generated_query\": generated_query,\n stream: bool,\n    ) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        question = question.strip()\n        if not self._passes_guardrail(question):\n            self._append_log(\n                stage=\"guardrail\",\n self._generate_answer(question, translation, raw_data)\n        relevant_node_ids = self._collect_relevant_node_ids(graph_result)\n        if memory is not None:\n            memory.add_turn(\"user\", return {\n            \"answer\": answer,\n            \"relevant_node_ids\": relevant_node_ids,\n            \"raw_data\": raw_data,\n            \"generated_query\":     return {\n            \"answer\": answer or self.REJECTION_MESSAGE,\n            \"relevant_node_ids\": [],\n            \"raw_data\": None,\n            \"generated_query\": None,\n prompt = (\n            f\"SCHEMA CONTEXT:\\n{schema_prompt}\\n\\n\"\n            f\"SQL SCHEMA:\\n{sql_schema_prompt}\\n\\n\"\n            f\"CONVERSATION HISTORY:\\n{conversation_context}\\n\\n\"\n
