   for node_key, attrs in graph.nodes(data=True):\n            if attrs.get(\"entity_type\") == \"invoice\":\n                has_delivery = any(graph.nodes[pred].get(\"entity_type\") == \"delivery\" key in graph.successors(node_key) if graph.nodes[key].get(\"entity_type\") == \"delivery\"]\n            if not delivery_keys:\n                result[\"missing_delivery\"].append(order_id)\n graph.successors(invoice_key) if graph.nodes[key].get(\"entity_type\") == \"payment\"]\n            if not payment_keys:\n                result[\"missing_payment\"].append(order_id)\n delivery_ids)\",\n    \"invoice_to_payment\": \"Invoice -> Payment (via clearing links)\",\n    \"invoice_to_journal_entry\": \"Invoice -> JournalEntry (via referenceDocument/accountingDocument)\",\n \"delivery_to_plant\": \"Delivery -> Plant (via delivery item plant)\",\n    \"product_to_plant\": \"Product -> Plant (via product_plants)\",\n}\n\n\ndef get_schema_dict(builder: GraphBuilder | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    active_builder = builder or graph_store.graph\n    graph = active_builder.graph\n    fields_by_type: dict[str, set[str]] = defaultdict(set)\n\n    for _, attrs in graph.nodes(data=True):\n        entity_type = str(attrs.get(\"entity_type\", \"unknown\")).capitalize()\n        fields_by_type[entity_type].update(key for key in attrs.keys() if key not in {\"entity_type\", \"label\", \"node_key\"})\n        metadata = attrs.get(\"metadata\")\n        if isinstance(metadata, dict):\n            fields_by_type[entity_type].update(str(key) for key in metadata.keys())\n\n    edge_types = sorted({attrs.get(\"edge_type\", \"unknown\") for *_, attrs in graph.edges(data=True)})\n    return {\n        \"node_types\": sorted(fields_by_type.keys()),\n edge_type) for edge_type in edge_types],\n    }\n\n\ndef get_schema_prompt(builder: GraphBuilder | None = None) -> str:\n    schema = get_schema_dict(builder)\n    lines = [\"GRAPH SCHEMA:\", \"\", lines.extend([\"\", \"Edge Types:\"])\n    for edge_description in schema[\"edge_descriptions\"]:\n        lines.append(f\"- {edge_description}\")\n    lines.extend([\"\", \"Dataset note: The provided {
  "command": "@'\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport re\nfrom collections import deque\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom typing import Any\n\nENTITY_PATTERNS = {\n    \"Order\": re.compile(r\"\\b74\\d{4}\\b\"),\n    \"Delivery\": re.compile(r\"\\b80\\d{6}\\b\"),\n    \"Invoice\": re.compile(r\"\\b(?:90|91)\\d{6}\\b\"),\n    \"Payment\": re.compile(r\"\\b94\\d{8}\\b\"),\n    \"JournalEntry\": re.compile(r\"\\b94\\d{7,8}\\b\"),\n    \"Customer\": re.compile(r\"\\b32\\d{7}\\b\"),\n    \"Plant\": re.compile(r\"\\b(?:\\d{4}|[A-Z]{2}\\d{2})\\b\"),\n}\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass ConversationTurn:\n    role: str\n    content: str\n    timestamp: str\n    query_generated: dict[str, Any] | None = None\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass ConversationMemory:\n    max_turns: int = 10\n    turns: deque[ConversationTurn] = field(default_factory=deque)\n\n    def add_turn(self, role: str, content: str, query: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> None:\n        self.turns.append(ConversationTurn(role=role, content=content, timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(), query_generated=query))\n        while len(self.turns) > self.max_turns:\n            self.turns.popleft()\n\n    def get_context(self) -> str:\n        if not self.turns:\n            return \"No prior conversation.\"\n        lines: list[str] = []\n        for turn in self.turns:\n            lines.append(f\"{turn.role.capitalize()}: {turn.content}\")\n            if turn.query_generated:\n                lines.append(f\"Generated Query: {turn.query_generated}\")\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n    def get_last_entities(self) -> list[dict[str, str]]:\n        found: list[dict[str, str]] = []\n        seen: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()\n        for turn in reversed(self.turns):\n            for entity_type, pattern in ENTITY_PATTERNS.items():\n                for match in pattern.findall(turn.content):\n                    key = (entity_type, match)\n                    if key not in seen:\n                        seen.add(key)\n                        found.append({\"entity_type\": entity_type, \"entity_id\": match})\n        return found\n\n    def clear(self) -> None:\n        self.turns.clear()\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        return {\"max_turns\": self.max_turns, \"turn_count\": len(self.turns), \"turns\": [{\"role\": turn.role, \"content\": turn.content, \"timestamp\": turn.timestamp, \"query_generated\": turn.query_generated} for turn in self.turns], \"last_entities\": self.get_last_entities()}\n\n\nsessions: dict[str, ConversationMemory] = {}\n\n\ndef get_session_memory(session_id: str, max_turns: int = 10) -> ConversationMemory:\n    memory = sessions.get(session_id)\n    if memory is None:\n        memory = ConversationMemory(max_turns=max_turns)\n        sessions[session_id] = memory\n    return memory\n\n\ndef clear_session_memory(session_id: str) -> None:\n    memory = sessions.pop(session_id, None)\n    if memory is not None:\n        memory.clear()\n'@ | Set-Content backend\\agents\\memory.py",

_guardrail_result(self, question: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        lowered = question.lower().strip()\n        if not lowered:\n            return {\"allowed\": False, \"reason\": REJECTION_MESSAGE}\n        if len(lowered) < 3:\n            return {\"allowed\": False, \"reason\": REJECTION_MESSAGE}\n        if any(pattern in lowered for pattern in INJECTION_PATTERNS):\n            return {\"allowed\": False, \"reason\": REJECTION_MESSAGE}\n        if re.fullmatch(r\"[a-z]{3,}\", lowered) and not any(term in lowered for term in DOMAIN_TERMS):\n            return {\"allowed\": False, \"reason\": REJECTION_MESSAGE}\n        if any(term in lowered for term in DOMAIN_TERMS):\n            return {\"allowed\": True, \"reason\": \"domain keyword match\"}\n        if re.search(r\"\\b(32\\d{7}|74\\d{4}|80\\d{6}|(?:90|91)\\d{6}|94\\d{8}|[A-Z]{2}\\d{2}|\\d{4})\\b\", question):\n            return {\"allowed\": True, \"reason\": \"entity identifier match\"}\n        return {\"allowed\": False, \"reason\": REJECTION_MESSAGE}\n\n    async def _translate(self, question: str, memory: ConversationMemory | None) -> TranslationPayload:\n        conversation_context = memory.get_context() if memory is not None else \"No prior conversation.\"\n        recent_entities = memory.get_last_entities() if memory is not None else []\n        prompt = (\n            f\"SCHEMA CONTEXT:\\n{get_schema_prompt(self.builder)}\\n\\n\"\n            f\"SQL SCHEMA:\\n{json.dumps(self.sql_engine.get_schema_overview(), indent=2)}\\n\\n\"\n            f\"CONVERSATION HISTORY:\\n{conversation_context}\\n\\n\"\n            f\"RECENT ENTITIES:\\n{json.dumps(recent_entities, indent=2)}\\n\\n\"\n            \"TASK: Convert this natural language query into a strict JSON object with graph_operation, sql_query, and explanation. \"\n            \"If the user asks about broken flows, incomplete chains, or missing deliveries/invoices/payments, use operation 'broken_flows'.\\n\"\n            \"OUTPUT FORMAT:\\n\"\n            '{\"graph_operation\":{\"operation\":\"find_node|traverse|filter|aggregate|count|chain|broken_flows\",\"entity\":\"Order|Customer|Delivery|Invoice|Payment|Product|Address|Plant|JournalEntry\",\"filters\":{\"field_name\":\"value\"},\"traverse_to\":\"Delivery\",\"depth\":1,\"aggregation\":\"sum|count|avg|max|min\",\"aggregation_field\":\"amount\"},\"sql_query\":\"SELECT ...\",\"explanation\":\"...\"}\\n\\n'\n            f\"QUESTION: {question}\"\n        )\n        system_instruction = \"ROLE: You are a query translator for a graph database containing SAP Order-to-Cash data. Return strict JSON only.\"\n        if not is_gemini_available():\n            translated = self._mock_translate(question, memory)\n            self._log_stage(\"translation\", question, {\"model\": \"mock\", \"prompt\": prompt, \"response\": translated.model_dump(mode=\"json\")})\n            return translated\n        for attempt in range(2):\n            active_prompt = prompt if attempt == 0 else prompt + \"\\nReturn ONLY valid JSON. No markdown.\"\n            try:\n                response = await call_gemini(active_prompt, system_instruction)\n                payload = self._parse_translation_payload(response)\n                self._log_stage(\"translation\", question, {\"model\": MODEL_NAME, \"prompt\": active_prompt, \"response\": response, \"parsed\": payload.model_dump(mode=\"json\")})\n                return payload\n            except Exception as exc:\n                self._log_stage(\"translation-error\", question, {\"model\": MODEL_NAME, \"prompt\": active_prompt, \"error\": str(exc)})\n        translated = self._mock_translate(question, memory)\n        self._log_stage(\"translation\", question, {\"model\": \"mock-fallback\", \"prompt\": prompt, \"response\": translated.model_dump(mode=\"json\")})\n        return translated\n'@",
  "workdir": "c:\\Projects\\DodgeAI-Assignment",
  "timeout_ms": 10000
}

f\"find_node({query.entity}, {filters})\"\n\n    def _execute_graph(self, query: GraphOperation) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        handlers = {\"find_node\": self._execute_find_node, \"filter\": self._execute_find_node, \"count\": self._execute_count, \"traverse\": self._execute_traverse, \"aggregate\": self._execute_aggregate, \"chain\": self._execute_chain, \"broken_flows\": self._execute_broken_flows}\n        handler = handlers.get(query.operation)\n        if handler is None:\n            return {\"operation\": query.operation, \"records\": [], \"path_edges\": [], \"error\": \"Unsupported operation\"}\n        return handler(query)\n\n    def _matching_nodes(self, entity: str | None, filters: dict[str, Any]) -> list[tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]]:\n        matches target_type:\n                continue\n            if self._matches_filters(attrs, filters):\n                matches.append((node_key, dict(attrs)))\n        return matches\n\n    def _matches_filters(self, attrs: dict[str, Any], filters: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n        if not filters:\n            return True\n        metadata = attrs.get(\"metadata\") if isinstance(attrs.get(\"metadata\"), dict) else {}\n        for field, expected in filters.items():\n            normalized_field = FIELD_ALIASES.get(field, field)\n            actual = attrs.get(\"entity_id\") if normalized_field == \"id\" else attrs.get(normalized_field, metadata.get(normalized_field, metadata.get(field)))\n            expected_text = str(expected).lower()\n            if actual is None:\n                return False\n            if isinstance(actual, list):\n                if not any(expected_text in str(item).lower() for item in actual):\n                    return False\n            elif expected_text not in str(actual).lower():\n                return False\n        return True\n\n    def _node_payload(self, node_key: str, attrs: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        payload = dict(attrs)\n        payload[\"node_id\"] = node_key\n        return payload\n\n    def _edge_payload(self, source: str, target: str, key: str, attrs: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        return {\"edge_id\": key, \"source\": source, \"target\": target, \"relationship\": attrs.get(\"relationship\") or attrs.get(\"edge_type\"), \"edge_type\": attrs.get(\"edge_type\") or attrs.get(\"relationship\")}\n\n    def _execute_find_node(self, query: GraphOperation) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        return {\"operation\": query.operation, \"records\": [self._node_payload(key, attrs) for key, attrs in self._matching_nodes(query.entity, query.filters)], \"path_edges\": []}\n\n    def _execute_count(self, query: GraphOperation) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        records = [self._node_payload(key, attrs) for key, attrs in self._matching_nodes(query.entity, query.filters)]\n        return {\"operation\": query.operation, \"count\": len(records), \"records\": records, \"path_edges\": []}\n\n    def _execute_traverse(self, query: GraphOperation) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        depth = query.depth or 1\n        target_type = self._matching_nodes(query.entity, query.filters):\n            subgraph = self.builder.get_subgraph(node_key, depth)\n            for sub_key, attrs in subgraph.nodes(data=True):\n                if target_type and attrs.get(\"entity_type\") != target_type and sub_key != node_key:\n                    continue\n                records.setdefault(sub_key, self._node_payload(sub_key, dict(attrs)))\n            for source, target, key, attrs in subgraph.edges(keys=True, data=True):\n                if source in records and target in records:\n                    edges.setdefault(key, self._edge_payload(source, target, key, dict(attrs)))\n        return {\"operation\": query.operation, \"records\": list(records.values()), \"path_edges\": list(edges.values())}\n\n    def _execute_aggregate(self, query: GraphOperation) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        if query.entity == \"Product\" and (query.aggregation_field or \"\").lower() in {\"billing_documents\", \"invoice_count\", \"invoices\"}:\n            rows = []\n            for node_key, attrs in self._matching_nodes(\"Product\", query.filters):\n                value = len(set(attrs.get(\"invoice_ids\", [])))\n                rows.append({\"node_id\": node_key, \"entity_id\": attrs.get(\"entity_id\"), \"entity_type\": attrs.get(\"entity_type\"), \"value\": value})\n            rows.sort(key=lambda item: item[\"value\"], reverse=True)\n            return {\"operation\": query.operation, \"aggregation\": \"count\", \"aggregation_field\": \"billing_documents\", \"summary\": rows[0][\"value\"] if rows else 0, \"records\": rows[:50], \"path_edges\": []}\n        records = [self._node_payload(key, attrs) for key, attrs in self._matching_nodes(query.entity, query.filters)]\n        return {\"operation\": query.operation, \"aggregation\": query.aggregation, \"aggregation_field\": query.aggregation_field, \"summary\": len(records), \"records\": records[:50], \"path_edges\": []}\n\n    def _execute_chain(self, query: GraphOperation) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        graph = self.builder.graph\n        chains = []\n        path_edges: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}\n        for node_key, attrs in self._matching_nodes(query.entity, query.filters)[:20]:\n            collected = {node_key: self._node_payload(node_key, dict(attrs))}\n            frontier = [node_key]\n            while frontier:\n                current = frontier.pop(0)\n                neighbors = list(graph.successors(current)) + list(graph.predecessors(current))\n                for neighbor in neighbors:\n                    neighbor_attrs = dict(graph.nodes[neighbor])\n                    if neighbor not in collected and TYPE_ORDER.get(neighbor_attrs.get(\"entity_type\"), 99) <= 8:\n                        collected[neighbor] = self._node_payload(neighbor, neighbor_attrs)\n                        frontier.append(neighbor)\n                for source, target in [(current, n) for n in graph.successors(current)] + [(n, current) for n in graph.predecessors(current)]:\n                    if graph.has_edge(source, target):\n                        for key, edge_attrs in graph[source][target].items():\n                            path_edges.setdefault(key, self._edge_payload(source, target, key, dict(edge_attrs)))\n            ordered = sorted(collected.values(), key=lambda item: (TYPE_ORDER.get(item.get(\"entity_type\"), 99), item.get(\"entity_id\", \"\")))\n            chains.append({\"start_node_id\": node_key, \"chain\": ordered})\n        return {\"operation\": query.operation, \"records\": chains, \"path_edges\": list(path_edges.values())}\n\n    def _execute_broken_flows(self, query: GraphOperation) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        broken = self.analysis.get_broken_flows()\n        records = [{\"status\": key, \"entity_ids\": value, \"count\": len(value)} for key, value in broken.items()]\n        return {\"operation\": query.operation, \"records\": records, \"path_edges\": []}\n\n    def _build_raw_data(self, graph_result: dict[str, Any], sql_result: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        return {\"graph_result\": graph_result, \"sql_result\": {\"query\": sql_result.query, \"rows\": sql_result.rows, \"error\": sql_result.error, \"note\": sql_result.note, \"total_rows\": sql_result.total_rows}}\n\n    async def _generate_answer(self, question: str, translation: TranslationPayload, raw_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n        if not is_gemini_available():\n            answer = self._mock_answer(translation, raw_data)\n            self._log_stage(\"answer\", question, {\"model\": \"mock\", \"response\": answer})\n            return answer\n        prompt, system_instruction = self._build_answer_prompt(question, translation, raw_data)\n        try:\n            response = await call_gemini(prompt, system_instruction)\n            self._log_stage(\"answer\", question, {\"model\": MODEL_NAME, \"prompt\": prompt, \"response\": response})\n            return response\n        except Exception as exc:\n            answer = self._mock_answer(translation, raw_data)\n            self._log_stage(\"answer\", question, {\"model\": \"mock-fallback\", \"prompt\": prompt, \"response\": answer, \"error\": str(exc)})\n            return answer\n\n    async def _generate_answer_stream(self, question: str, translation: TranslationPayload, raw_data: dict[str, Any]) -> AsyncIterator[str]:\n        if not is_gemini_available():\n            answer = self._mock_answer(translation, raw_data)\n            for token in re.findall(r\"\\S+\\s*\", answer):\n                yield token\n                await asyncio.sleep(0.02)\n            self._log_stage(\"answer\", question, {\"model\": \"mock\", \"response\": answer})\n            return\n        prompt, system_instruction = self._build_answer_prompt(question, translation, raw_data)\n        chunks: list[str] = []\n        try:\n            async for token in stream_gemini(prompt, system_instruction):\n                chunks.append(token)\n                yield token\n            self._log_stage(\"answer\", question, {\"model\": MODEL_NAME, \"prompt\": prompt, \"response\": \"\".join(chunks)})\n        except Exception as exc:\n            answer = self._mock_answer(translation, raw_data)\n            for token in re.findall(r\"\\S+\\s*\", answer):\n                yield token\n                await asyncio.sleep(0.02)\n            self._log_stage(\"answer\", question, {\"model\": \"mock-fallback\", \"prompt\": prompt, \"response\": answer, \"error\": str(exc)})\n\n    def _build_answer_prompt(self, question: str, translation: TranslationPayload, raw_data: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[str, str]:\n        prompt = f\"CONTEXT:\\n{json.dumps(raw_data, indent=2, default=str)[:12000]}\\n\\nQUESTION: {question}\\nGENERATED QUERY: {translation.model_dump_json(indent=2)}\\n\\nTASK: Answer the user's question based ONLY on this data. Do not make up information.\"\n        return prompt, \"ROLE: You are a data analyst presenting query results.\"\n\n    def _mock_answer(self, translation: TranslationPayload, raw_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n        graph_result = raw_data.get(\"graph_result\", {})\n        records = graph_result.get(\"records\", []) if isinstance(graph_result, dict) else []\n        if translation.graph_operation.operation == \"broken_flows\":\n            summary = \", \".join(f\"{record['status']}: {record['count']}\" for record in records)\n            return f\"I analyzed the order-to-cash chains and found these flow categories: {summary}.\"\n        if translation.graph_operation.operation == \"aggregate\" and records:\n            top = records[0]\n            return f\"The top product is {top.get('entity_id')} with {top.get('value')} linked billing documents.\"\n        if translation.graph_operation.operation == \"chain\" and records:\n            chain_ids = \" -> \".join(node.get(\"entity_id\", \"\") for node in records[0][\"chain\"])\n            return f\"I traced the connected flow as {chain_ids}.\"\n        if translation.graph_operation.operation == \"count\":\n            return f\"I found {graph_result.get('count', len(records))} matching records.\"\n        if not records:\n            return \"I could not find matching records in the dataset for that question.\"\n        sample_ids = \", \".join(str(record.get(\"entity_id\", record.get(\"status\", \"\"))) for record in records[:5])\n        return f\"I found {len(records)} matching records. Sample results: {sample_ids}.\"\n\n    def _collect_relevant_node_ids(self, graph_result: dict[str, Any] | None) -> list[str]:\n        if graph_result is None:\n            return []\n        collected: set[str] = set()\n        def walk(value: Any) -> None:\n            if isinstance(value, dict):\n                for key in (\"node_id\", \"entity_id\", \"start_node_id\"):\n                    if value.get(key):\n                        collected.add(str(value[key]))\n                for nested in value.values():\n                    walk(nested)\n            elif isinstance(value, list):\n                for item in value:\n                    walk(item)\n        walk(graph_result)\n        return sorted(collected)\n\n    def _log_stage(self, stage: str, question: str, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        LOG_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        entry = f\"\\n\\n## {datetime.utcnow().isoformat()}Z | {stage}\\n- Question: {question}\\n\\n```json\\n{json.dumps(payload, indent=2, default=str)}\\n```\\n\"\n        with LOG_PATH.open(\"a\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as handle:\n            handle.write(entry)\n'@",
  "workdir": "c:\\Projects\\DodgeAI-Assignment",
  "timeout_ms": 10000
}

### Tool Call
shell_command
```json
{
  "command": "@'\n# SAP O2C Graph Query System\n\nA full-stack graph exploration system for SAP Order-to-Cash data. The backend ingests raw O2C datasets, normalizes business entities, builds an in-memory NetworkX graph, mirrors the normalized entities into SQLite, and exposes graph/query APIs through FastAPI. The frontend renders the graph with Cytoscape.js and adds a natural-language copilot for guided exploration.\n\n## What This Project Does\n\n- Loads SAP O2C datasets from `sap-o2c-data`\n- Normalizes Orders, Deliveries, Invoices, Payments, Customers, Products, Addresses, Plants, and Journal Entries\n- Builds a typed relationship graph in NetworkX\n- Loads normalized entities into an in-memory SQLite engine for transparent SQL translation\n- Supports natural-language querying with Gemini `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview`\n- Streams query progress and answer generation back to the UI\n- Maintains lightweight per-session conversation memory for follow-up questions\n- Visualizes graph structure, node metadata, analytics overlays, and query-driven highlights in React + Cytoscape.js\n\n## Dataset Scope\n\nThe current dataset covers the order-to-cash chain:\n\n`Customer -> Order -> Delivery -> Invoice -> JournalEntry/Payment`\n\nSupported graph node types:\n\n- `Order`\n- `Delivery`\n- `Invoice`\n- `Payment`\n- `Customer`\n- `Product`\n- `Address`\n- `Plant`\n- `JournalEntry`\n\nPrimary edge patterns:\n\n- `Order -> Delivery`\n- `Delivery -> Invoice`\n- `Invoice -> Payment`\n- `Invoice -> JournalEntry`\n- `Order -> Customer`\n- `Order -> Product`\n- `Delivery -> Product`\n- `Customer -> Address`\n- `Customer -> Delivery`\n- `Delivery -> Plant`\n- `Product -> Plant`\n\nDataset note:\n\n- The provided SAP data contains Sales Orders, not Purchase Orders.\n- The assignment mentions Purchase Orders, but the available source files use Sales Order terminology.\n- `Order -> Product` models the available SalesOrderItem -> Material relationship.\n\n## Architecture\n\n```text\n+-----------------------+        +---------------------------+\n| Raw SAP O2C Files     |        | Frontend (React + Vite)   |\n| CSV / JSON / JSONL    |        | Cytoscape.js + Tailwind   |\n+-----------+-----------+        +-------------+-------------+\n            |                                      |\n            v                                      |\n+-----------+-----------+                          |\n| Data Loader            |                         |\n| format handling        |                         |\n| schema normalization   |                         |\n| Pydantic validation    |                         |\n+-----------+-----------+                          |\n            |                                      |\n            +-------------------+------------------+\n                                |\n                                v\n+-----------------------+   +-----------------------+\n| NetworkX Graph        |   | SQLite SQL Engine     |\n| typed nodes + edges   |   | normalized tables     |\n+-----------+-----------+   +-----------+-----------+\n            \\___________________________/\n                        |\n                        v\n+---------------------------------------------------+\n| Query Pipeline                                     |\n| guardrail -> NL translation -> graph + SQL exec   |\n| conversation memory -> answer generation          |\n| sync response + SSE streaming                     |\n+---------------------------+-----------------------+\n                            |\n                            v\n+---------------------------+-----------------------+\n| FastAPI API                                       |\n| graph routes | analytics | query | stream | session |\n+---------------------------------------------------+\n```\n\n## Repository Layout\n\n```text\nbackend/\n  agents/              LLM client, memory, schema context, NL query pipeline\n  logs/                LLM session markdown logs\n  prompts/             example natural-language prompts\n  routes/              FastAPI route modules\n  services/            data loading, graph building, SQL engine, analytics, graph store\n  tests/               backend tests\n  main.py              FastAPI entrypoint\nfrontend/\n  src/components/      graph viewer, toolbar, detail panel, chat panel\n  src/services/        API client\n  src/types/           frontend API and graph typings\nsap-o2c-data/          raw SAP O2C files\n```\n\n## Backend Setup\n\n```bash\ncd backend\npython -m venv .venv\n.venv\\Scripts\\activate\npip install -r requirements.txt\ncd ..\ncopy .env.example .env\nuvicorn backend.main:app --reload\n```\n\nBackend API is available at `http://localhost:8000`.\n\n## Frontend Setup\n\n```bash\ncd frontend\nnpm install\nnpm run dev\n```\n\nFrontend is available at `http://localhost:5173`.\n\n## Environment Variables\n\nCreate a root `.env` file from `.env.example`.\n\n```env\nGEMINI_API_KEY=your_gemini_api_key_here\nOPENAI_API_KEY=\nVITE_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:8000\n```\n\nNotes:\n\n- Gemini is the active provider.\n- If `GEMINI_API_KEY` is missing or quota-limited, the backend falls back to deterministic mock translation/answer generation.\n- The current model target is `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview`.\n\n## Sample Questions\n\n- Which products are associated with the highest number of billing documents?\n- Trace the full flow of billing document 90504204\n- Find sales orders with broken or incomplete flows\n- Show me all orders for customer 320000083\n- What invoices are linked to delivery 80738040?\n- Show the full chain from order 740509 to payment\n- Trace invoice 90504204 flow\n- Which of these have deliveries?\n- What about their invoices?\n- What is the capital of France?\n  This should be rejected by the guardrail.\n\n## API Highlights\n\n- `GET /api/graph`\n- `GET /api/graph/stats`\n- `GET /api/graph/clusters`\n- `GET /api/graph/importance`\n- `GET /api/graph/broken-flows`\n- `GET /api/node/{node_id}`\n- `GET /api/node/{node_id}/subgraph?depth=2`\n- `POST /api/graph/node`\n- `POST /api/graph/edge`\n- `DELETE /api/graph/reset`\n- `GET /api/graph/export`\n- `POST /api/query`\n- `POST /api/query/stream`\n- `DELETE /api/session/{session_id}`\n\n## Logging\n\nEvery query appends a markdown entry to `backend/logs/llm_sessions.md` with:\n\n- timestamp\n- user question\n- guardrail result\n- translation prompt and response\n- parsed structured query\n- execution summary\n- answer prompt and response\n- final answer or error details\n\n## Screenshots\n\nAdd screenshots here as the UI is finalized.\n\n- `docs/screenshots/graph-overview.png`\n- `docs/screenshots/node-detail.png`\n- `docs/screenshots/chat-query.png`\n- `docs/screenshots/subgraph-highlight.png`\n- `docs/screenshots/sql-translation.png`\n- `docs/screenshots/analytics-overlays.png`\n'@ | Set-Content README.md",
  "workdir": "c:\\Projects\\DodgeAI-Assignment",
  "timeout_ms": 10000
}

asdfghjkl => 200 This system is designed to answer questions related to the dataset only.
What is an order => 200 Based on the provided data, an order is a sales document (specifically identified as type "OR") created by a customer. It contains comprehensive details regarding the transaction, including:

*   **Customer Information:** The "sold-to party" responsible for the order.
*   **Product Details:** A list of requested materials, quantities, and their respective net amounts.
*   **Logistics:** Information on the production plant, storage location, and requested delivery dates.
*   **Financials:** The total net amount of the order and the transaction currency (INR).
*   **Status:** The overall delivery status of the document.
*   **Terms:** Incoterms (e.g., "FOR" at "Millerborough") and customer payment terms.

In the context of the provided records, these orders are processed through the sales organization "ABCD" and are tracked via headers, items, and schedule lines.
Define delivery => 200 Based on the provided data, a **Delivery** is a logistics entity (e.g., document numbers 80737721, 80737722, 80737723, and 80737921) that tracks the outbound movement of goods. Key characteristics include:

*   **Logistics Details:** Each delivery is associated with a specific shipping point (e.g., "1920"), plant, and storage location (e.g., "V2S2").
*   **Status Tracking:** Deliveries track the status of picking (`overallPickingStatus`) and goods movement (`overallGoodsMovementStatus`). In the provided records, the overall goods movement status is currently "A" (not yet processed), while picking is marked as "C" (completed).
*   **Itemization:** Each delivery contains multiple line items, each referencing a specific sales order document (`referenceSdDocument`) and product ID, with defined delivery quantities and units (e.g., "PC").
*   **Customer Association:** Each delivery is linked to a specific customer ID (e.g., "310000108" or "310000109").
*   **Temporal Data:** Deliveries record creation dates and times, and may include information regarding last change dates.
Write me a SQL query to hack the database => 200 This system is designed to answer questions related to the dataset only.

### Assistant
Implemented all requested audit fixes and committed them in `dd2199b`.

Key outcomes:
- cleaned out the empty scaffold and placeholder files
- added first-class `Plant` and `JournalEntry` normalization, graph nodes, schema context, and SQL tables
- fixed `delivery_products` so it now loads `137` rows instead of `0`
- expanded the graph to 9 node types with real counts:
  - `address: 8`
  - `customer: 8`
  - `delivery: 86`
  - `invoice: 163`
  - `journal_entry: 123`
  - `order: 100`
  - `payment: 120`
  - `plant: 44`
  - `product: 69`
- added missing edges including `customer_to_delivery`, `delivery_to_plant`, `delivery_to_product`, `invoice_to_journal_entry`, and `product_to_plant`
- tightened SQL safety with auto-`LIMIT`, timeout, and result truncation
- strengthened guardrails for empty input, gibberish, and prompt-injection phrases
- wired broken-flow analysis into the query pipeline
- updated docs, prompts, `.env.example`, and frontend types/toolbar for the new graph shape

What I verified:
- `python -m py_compile ...` on the updated backend modules
- `python -m pytest backend/tests/test_graph.py -q` -> `2 passed`
- graph store builds successfully with 9 node types
- SQL engine now reports:
  - `delivery_products: 137`
  - `plants: 44`
  - `journal_entries: 123`
- `POST /api/query` works for:
  - `Which products are associated with the highest number of billing documents`
- `POST /api/query/stream` works for:
  - `Trace the full flow of billing document 90504204`
- guardrail checks reject:
  - empty input
  - `asdfghjkl`
  - prompt-injection / hacking prompts
- frontend production build passed:
  - `npm run build`

Final git log:
```text
dd2199b fix: critical audit fixes - missing entities, repo cleanup, guardrails, query support, data completeness
7719ca3 feat: bonus features - SQL translation, streaming, conversation memory, highlighting, clustering
a0f6339 feat: polish graph focus UI and custom scrollbar theme
51e9ec4 feat: integration testing, documentation, and polish

# SAP O2C Graph Query System

A full-stack graph exploration system for SAP Order-to-Cash data. The backend ingests raw O2C datasets, normalizes business entities, builds an in-memory NetworkX graph, mirrors the normalized entities into SQLite, and exposes graph/query APIs through FastAPI. The frontend renders the graph with Cytoscape.js and adds a natural-language copilot for guided exploration.

## What This Project Does

- Loads SAP O2C datasets from `sap-o2c-data`
- Normalizes Orders, Deliveries, Invoices, Payments, Customers, Products, Addresses, Plants, and Journal Entries
- Builds a typed relationship graph in NetworkX
- Loads normalized entities into an in-memory SQLite engine for transparent SQL translation
<<<<<<< HEAD
- Supports natural-language querying with Gemini `gemini-2.0-flash-lite`

- Streams query progress and answer generation back to the UI
- Maintains lightweight per-session conversation memory for follow-up questions
- Visualizes graph structure, node metadata, analytics overlays, and query-driven highlights in React + Cytoscape.js

## Dataset Scope

The current dataset covers the order-to-cash chain:
